title: Divorce Settlement Cost Estimator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Free divorce cost calculator for Argentina. Estimate attorney fees, liquidation costs, and timelines for marital property division.
intro: Divorce proceedings in Argentina have variable costs depending on jurisdiction, processing timelines, and required documentation. Understanding these costs upfront saves time and prevents unexpected expenses. Our 2026 divorce cost estimator factors attorney fees, court costs, and procedural requirements. Values are updated regularly to keep you informed of current legal costs.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **asset value + attorney fees**. Direct calculation based on your inputs. Whether uncontested or contested, get an estimated cost and timeline for your situation.
useCases: Technical calculations related to divorce settlement costs
Validate cost estimates before consulting an attorney
Quick reference for financial planning during separation
explanation:
**Official divorce proceedings** in Argentina have costs, timelines, and requirements defined by each government agency (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Migraciones, Civil Registry, etc.). This calculator estimates your total costs and necessary documentation.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusually high or low values based on Argentine legal standards.

## Important Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference only. For critical decisions involving finances, property division, or family law matters, consult with a licensed attorney specializing in Argentine divorce law. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically for accuracy.

faq.q: What's the difference between uncontested and contested divorce?
faq.a: Uncontested: Both parties agree on terms. Faster (about 3 months) and significantly less expensive. Contested: Disagreement on property division, custody, or support. Can take 1-3 years with higher legal costs.

faq.q: How much do divorce attorneys charge in Argentina?
faq.a: Attorney fees typically range from 5-15% of marital property value. If there are minimal assets, expect a base fee of approximately $500k-2M ARS.

faq.q: Does having children increase divorce costs?
faq.a: Yes. Child support calculations and custody arrangements add legal complexity and work, significantly increasing both attorney fees and overall timeline.

faq.q: What is express divorce in Argentina?
faq.a: Since the 2015 Civil and Commercial Code reform, express divorce requires no grounds—either spouse can file unilaterally. Minimum timeline is 3 months regardless of assets or children.

faq.q: Is mediation required before divorce in Argentina?
faq.a: Yes. Mediation is mandatory in most family law cases before court proceedings. This is a required procedural step in Argentine divorce law.

faq.q: Do I need an appointment to file for divorce?
faq.a: Most jurisdictions require an appointment, available online through argentina.gob.ar or the relevant government agency website. Walk-in visits are only permitted for urgent cases.

faq.q: Can I represent myself in an Argentine divorce?
faq.a: For uncontested divorces with simple property splits, self-representation is possible, but it's risky. An attorney ensures proper documentation and protects your interests and rights.

faq.q: How long does divorce take in Argentina?
faq.a: Uncontested divorce: 1-3 months. Contested divorce: 1-3 years depending on complexity, court availability, and whether children are involved.
